name: Uracil mustard
description: Nitrogen mustard derivative of uracil. It is a alkylating antineoplastic agent that is used in lymphatic malignancies, and causes mainly gastrointestinal and bone marrow damage. [PubChem]
mechanismOfAction: After activation, it binds preferentially to the guanine and cytosine moieties of DNA, leading to cross-linking of DNA, thus inhibiting DNA synthesis and function.
pharmacodynamics: Uracil Mustard selectively inhibits the synthesis of deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA). The guanine and cytosine content correlates with the degree of Uracil Mustard-induced cross-linking. At high concentrations of the drug, cellular RNA and protein synthesis are also suppressed.

interactions.name: Trastuzumab
interactions.description: Trastuzumab may increase the cardiotoxic activities of Uracil mustard.

interactions.name: Paclitaxel
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Paclitaxel is combined with Uracil mustard.

interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Docetaxel is combined with Uracil mustard.

interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Cabazitaxel is combined with Uracil mustard.
